headline: Wiley Joins U.S. Department of Energy's Genesis Mission Consortium to Advance AI-Powered Scientific Discovery
summary: HOBOKEN, N.J., July 22, 2026--Wiley (NYSE: WLY) today announced its membership in the Genesis Mission Consortium, the public-private partnership supporting the U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Genesis Mission, joining organizations including Nvidia, AWS, Microsoft, IBM, and AMD. Wiley is the only scientific publisher among the consortium's members and has been invited to demonstrate its work today at the Genesis Mission Annual Summit in Washington, D.C.

content.title: Wiley Joins U.S. Department of Energy's Genesis Mission Consortium to Advance AI-Powered Scientific Discovery
content.summary: HOBOKEN, N.J., July 22, 2026--Wiley (NYSE: WLY) today announced its membership in the Genesis Mission Consortium, the public-private partnership supporting the U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Genesis Mission, joining organizations including Nvidia, AWS, Microsoft, IBM, and AMD. Wiley is the only scientific publisher among the consortium's members and has been invited to demonstrate its work today at the Genesis Mission Annual Summit in Washington, D.C.

headline: IBM cuts yearly revenue growth forecast as customers prioritize AI infrastructure spending
summary: IBM cut its annual revenue growth forecast on Wednesday, days after shocking Wall Street with a warning that corporate spending was shifting toward AI-focused data-center ‌gear at the expense of its software and mainframe computers.  The company also missed profit and revenue ‌expectations for the second quarter ended June 30.  Executives sought to reassure shareholders that customers prioritized spending on AI in the quarter ​but were not looking to move away from mainframes in the longer term.

content.title: IBM cuts yearly revenue growth forecast as customers prioritize AI infrastructure spending

Executives sought to reassure shareholders that customers prioritized spending on AI in the quarter ​but were not looking to move away from mainframes in the longer term.

headline: IBM (NYSE:IBM) Misses Q2 CY2026 Sales Expectations
summary: Technology and consulting giant IBM (NYSE:IBM) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026 as sales only rose 1.1% year on year to $17.16 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.93 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: IBM (NYSE:IBM) Misses Q2 CY2026 Sales Expectations
content.summary: Technology and consulting giant IBM (NYSE:IBM) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026 as sales only rose 1.1% year on year to $17.16 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.93 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: IBM cuts annual revenue growth forecast as customers prioritize AI infrastructure spending
summary: IBM cut its annual revenue growth forecast on Wednesday, days after shocking Wall Street with a warning that corporate spending was shifting toward AI-focused data-center gear at ‌the expense of its software and mainframe computers.  Executives sought to reassure shareholders that customers prioritized spending on AI in the quarter but were not looking ​to move away from mainframes in the longer term.  CEO Arvind Krishna said last week IBM had "faltered" in adapting and "numerous large deals" had slipped, sending the company's shares down 25%, its steepest one-day fall in more than a century.

content.title: IBM cuts annual revenue growth forecast as customers prioritize AI infrastructure spending
content.summary: IBM cut its annual revenue growth forecast on Wednesday, days after shocking Wall Street with a warning that corporate spending was shifting toward AI-focused data-center gear at ‌the expense of its software and mainframe computers.  Executives sought to reassure shareholders that customers prioritized spending on AI in the quarter but were not looking ​to move away from mainframes in the longer term.  CEO Arvind Krishna said last week IBM had "faltered" in adapting and "numerous large deals" had slipped, sending the company's shares down 25%, its steepest one-day fall in more than a century.
